question.label: Executor mode
question.name: Where are you still operating more like an executor than a strategic owner?
question.description: Identifies the parts of his work that remain tactical when they may need broader ownership.

answer: A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.

assessment.note: Names a missing structure — a clear growth contract with milestones — which signals a strong growth investment gap and some clarity about what would help.

new_queue.label: Next move
new_queue.name: What is the first concrete move you want to make before we next meet?

new_queue.label: Next move
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to focus first?

history.answer_excerpt: I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.
